stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • From Earth, the meteors in a meteor shower appear to stream outward from one small region of the sky.
  • That region is called the meteor shower’s radiant.
  • The Leonid meteor shower is visible across much of Earth in November.
  • Most meteor showers, including the Leonids, are named after the location of their radiant.
  • The Leonid radiant lies within the constellation Leo.
stem: The student wants to explain the origin of the Leonid meteor shower’s name. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The Leonid meteor shower, which has a radiant, is visible across much of Earth in November.
From Earth, the meteors in a meteor shower appear to stream outward from a radiant, such as the one in Leo.
Most meteor showers are named after a region of the sky.
The Leonid meteor shower is named for the constellation Leo, the location of the meteor shower’s radiant.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence explains the origin of the Leonid meteor shower’s name: the constellation Leo, where the shower’s radiant is located.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence indicates when the Leonids are visible; it doesn’t explain the origin of the name. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence discusses meteor showers in general; it doesn’t explain the origin of the Leonids’ name specifically. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence makes a general claim about meteor shower names; it doesn’t explain the origin of the Leonids’ name.
